items.title: Fantasy Basketball Rankings: Top-40 rookies for dynasty formats following Las Vegas Summer League
items.summary: The NBA Summer League wrapped up in Las Vegas on Sunday with the Golden State Warriors taking home the championship, and rookie Yaxel Lendenborg winning MVP honors. While it's always dangerous to overreact to a handful of games against inconsistent competition, it gave us our first real look at how this rookie class translates to the NBA level.This top 40 is built for 10- and 12-team leagues running three-round rookie drafts (36 picks), with a handful of extra names thrown in for depth. It's a deep class once again and there will certainly be some surprises — especially at the top of the board. Let's get to it.Rookie rankings: The top 101. Caleb Wilson - F/C,  Chicago BullsWe're coming out swinging. Wilson over the consensus first pick, Cameron Boozer. I think Wilson has the highest ceiling in the class. Scoring, rebounding, stocks, 3s and a dog mentality — he looks…

items.title: LeBron James free agency: Making the case for each finalist
items.summary: NEW YORK, NEW YORK - JULY 16: LeBron James speaks onstage during the TIME100 Sports Gala at Pier 60, Chelsea Piers on July 16, 2026 in New York City. (Photo by Craig Barritt/Getty Images for TIME) | Getty Images for TIME LeBron James’ next team remains a mystery. The new iteration of “The Decision” has more or less left everyone in the dark, and has an extremely different air than past choices. Rather than being the nexus of a championship team, the 41-year-old iteration of LeBron instead serves as the indispensable veteran, the glue guy, someone who gets added to be the third option on an established contender to push them over the hump. There’s incredible appeal for his skills with so many teams mortgaging the future to win right now, and if LeBron is the final piece of the puzzle, he’ll be well worth it.Now we dive into the six teams who seem to be the favorites to land LeBron and…

items.title: What are the next steps for Nate Ament?
items.summary: LAS VEGAS, NEVADA - JULY 14: (Editors Note: Tonal effects have been applied to this image) Nate Ament #15 of the Milwaukee Bucks poses for a portrait during the 2026 NBA Rookie Photo Shoot at UNLV on July 14, 2026 in Las Vegas, Nevada. (Photo by Carmen Mandato/Getty Images) | Getty Images With Summer League wrapped up, I wanted to discuss Nate Ament and how he moves forward from here. Ament is certainly one of the more difficult prospect evaluations I’ve ever done (not that I’m some expert in this space) because he is a truly unique archetype insofar as he has me thinking “OK, this is certainly a unique skillset, but it doesn’t translate to winning basketball as of yet; how does he get there?” To start, I think the most prudent thing to do is to lay out what I believe are his strengths and weaknesses right now.Player breakdownStrengths Spot-up shooting/attacking hard closeoutsBodying…

items.title: Roster continuity deserves a place in the next CBA
items.summary: NEW YORK, NY - JUNE 25: Devin Booker looks on after being selected 13th overall by the Phoenix Suns in the First Round of the 2015 NBA Draft at the Barclays Center on June 25, 2015 in the Brooklyn borough of New York City. NOTE TO USER: User expressly acknowledges and agrees that, by downloading and or using this photograph, User is consenting to the terms and conditions of the Getty Images License Agreement. (Photo by Elsa/Getty Images) | Getty Images I began watching the NBA in 1988, nearly 40 years ago now. Granted, I experienced it through the eyes of a child who was captivated by the game, but I was fortunate enough to grow up watching not only an entertaining brand of basketball but one built on consistency.Free agency truly arrived the season after I became a fan, and trades have always been part of the league. Even so, continuity was still the norm. Players moved when…

(Photo by Louis Grasse/Getty Images) | Getty Images Las Vegas Summer League is the most wonderful time of the year. It’s a time when every dribble, rebound, and shot can mean as much or as little as the beholder wants. My personal rulebook for Summer League is that rookies can only improve their stock; second-year guys should look like second-year guys; and third-year guys better not play poorly, or they will be looking for a spot on my hometown professional team,…

items.title: Kam Williams is now in a medical boot with crutches
items.summary: LEXINGTON, KY - FEBRUARY 07: Guard Kam Williams (3) of the Kentucky Wildcats before a game between the Tennessee Volunteers and the Kentucky Wildcats on February 7, 2026, at Rupp Arena in Lexington, KY. (Photo by Jeff Moreland/Icon Sportswire via Getty Images) | Icon Sportswire via Getty Images If you have spotted Kentucky Basketball forward Kam Williams around campus recently, you probably noticed a couple of familiar summer accessories: a protective walking boot and crutches, as seen at the TBT.Before Big Blue Nation lets out a collective sigh of panic, take a deep breath. This isn’t a case of the injury bug striking again, but rather a visual reminder of the strict, ultra-cautious rehabilitation program Williams has been on all offseason. As Mark Pope and his staff look ahead to the 2026-27 campaign, the goal is simple: zero rushing, zero risks, and ensuring Williams is at absolute…
